pcre2.c(3) Library Functions Manual pcre2.c(3)

NAME

pcre2.c - Centralized PCRE2 singleton pattern for efficient regex compilation.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct pcre2_singleton
Represents a thread-safe compiled PCRE2 regex singleton.

Typedefs


typedef struct pcre2_singleton pcre2_singleton_t
Represents a thread-safe compiled PCRE2 regex singleton.

Functions


pcre2_singleton_t * asciichat_pcre2_singleton_compile (const char *pattern, uint32_t flags)
Compile and cache a PCRE2 regex pattern with thread-safe singleton semantics. pcre2_code * asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code (pcre2_singleton_t *singleton)
Get the compiled pcre2_code from a singleton handle. bool asciichat_pcre2_singleton_is_initialized (pcre2_singleton_t *singleton)
Check if a singleton was successfully initialized. void asciichat_pcre2_singleton_free (pcre2_singleton_t *singleton)
Free a PCRE2 singleton and its resources. void asciichat_pcre2_cleanup_all (void)
Free all PCRE2 singletons in the global registry. char * asciichat_pcre2_extract_named_group (pcre2_code *regex, pcre2_match_data *match_data, const char *group_name, const char *subject)
Extract named substring from PCRE2 match data. char * asciichat_pcre2_extract_group (pcre2_match_data *match_data, int group_num, const char *subject)
Extract numbered capture group as allocated string. const char * asciichat_pcre2_extract_group_ptr (pcre2_match_data *match_data, int group_num, const char *subject, size_t *out_len)
Extract numbered capture group as pointer into subject (non-allocating). bool asciichat_pcre2_extract_group_ulong (pcre2_match_data *match_data, int group_num, const char *subject, unsigned long *out_value)
Extract numbered capture group and convert to unsigned long.

Detailed Description

Centralized PCRE2 singleton pattern for efficient regex compilation.

Definition in file pcre2.c.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct pcre2_singleton pcre2_singleton_t

Represents a thread-safe compiled PCRE2 regex singleton. Uses lifecycle_t for thread-safe lazy initialization without mutexes. The compilation flag is set only once, and the compiled code is read-only after that, enabling concurrent access from multiple threads.

Function Documentation

void asciichat_pcre2_cleanup_all (void )

Free all PCRE2 singletons in the global registry. Walks the global registry and frees all singletons. Safe to call multiple times (idempotent). Should be called once during shutdown.

Definition at line 210 of file pcre2.c.

210                                        {
211   if (!lifecycle_shutdown(&g_registry_lc)) {
212     return; /* No singletons were ever created or already cleaned up */
213   }
214 
215   /* Check if already cleaned up */
216   if (g_singleton_registry == NULL) {
217     return;
218   }
219 
220   pcre2_singleton_t *current = g_singleton_registry;
221 
222   /* Clear registry first to prevent re-entry */
223   g_singleton_registry = NULL;
224 
225   /* Now free all singletons */
226   while (current) {
227     pcre2_singleton_t *next = current->next;
228 
229     /* Free compiled code */
230     pcre2_code *code = (pcre2_code *)(uintptr_t)atomic_load_u64(&current->code);
231     if (code) {
232       pcre2_code_free(code);
233       atomic_store_u64(&current->code, 0);
234     }
235 
236     /* Free JIT stack */
237     if (current->jit_stack) {
238       pcre2_jit_stack_free(current->jit_stack);
239       current->jit_stack = NULL;
240     }
241 
242     /* Free pattern string */
243     SAFE_FREE(current->pattern);
244 
245     /* Free singleton structure */
246     SAFE_FREE(current);
247 
248     current = next;
249   }
250 }




References atomic_load_u64(), atomic_store_u64(),
    pcre2_singleton::code, pcre2_singleton::jit_stack,
    lifecycle_shutdown(), pcre2_singleton::next,
    pcre2_singleton::pattern, and SAFE_FREE.


Referenced by asciichat_shared_destroy(), and
    main().






char
  * asciichat_pcre2_extract_group (pcre2_match_data * match_data, int group_num,
  const char * subject)


Extract numbered capture group as allocated string.


Definition at line 303 of file pcre2.c.


303                                                                                                       {
304   if (!match_data || !subject || group_num < 0) {
305     return NULL;
306   }
307 
308   /* Get ovector (output vector with match offsets) */
309   PCRE2_SIZE *ovector = pcre2_get_ovector_pointer(match_data);
310   PCRE2_SIZE start = ovector[2 * group_num];
311   PCRE2_SIZE end = ovector[2 * group_num + 1];
312 
313   if (start == PCRE2_UNSET || end == PCRE2_UNSET) {
314     return NULL; /* Group not matched */
315   }
316 
317   /* Allocate and copy substring */
318   size_t len = end - start;
319   char *result = SAFE_MALLOC(len + 1, char *);
320   if (!result) {
321     log_error("asciichat_pcre2_extract_group: failed to allocate %zu bytes", len + 1);
322     return NULL;
323   }
324 
325   memcpy(result, subject + start, len);
326   result[len] = '\0';
327 
328   return result;
329 }






References SAFE_MALLOC.






const
  char * asciichat_pcre2_extract_group_ptr (pcre2_match_data * match_data, int
  group_num, const char * subject, size_t * out_len)


Extract numbered capture group as pointer into subject
    (non-allocating).


Definition at line 334 of file pcre2.c.


335                                                                {
336   if (!match_data || !subject || !out_len || group_num < 0) {
337     return NULL;
338   }
339 
340   /* Get ovector (output vector with match offsets) */
341   PCRE2_SIZE *ovector = pcre2_get_ovector_pointer(match_data);
342   PCRE2_SIZE start = ovector[2 * group_num];
343   PCRE2_SIZE end = ovector[2 * group_num + 1];
344 
345   if (start == PCRE2_UNSET || end == PCRE2_UNSET) {
346     return NULL; /* Group not matched */
347   }
348 
349   *out_len = end - start;
350   return subject + start;
351 }






Referenced by stun_servers_parse().






bool asciichat_pcre2_extract_group_ulong (pcre2_match_data
  * match_data, int group_num, const char * subject, unsigned long *
  out_value)


Extract numbered capture group and convert to unsigned long.


Definition at line 356 of file pcre2.c.


357                                                                    {
358   if (!match_data || !subject || !out_value || group_num < 0) {
359     return false;
360   }
361 
362   /* Get ovector (output vector with match offsets) */
363   PCRE2_SIZE *ovector = pcre2_get_ovector_pointer(match_data);
364   PCRE2_SIZE start = ovector[2 * group_num];
365   PCRE2_SIZE end = ovector[2 * group_num + 1];
366 
367   if (start == PCRE2_UNSET || end == PCRE2_UNSET) {
368     return false; /* Group not matched */
369   }
370 
371   /* Extract substring into temporary buffer */
372   size_t len = end - start;
373   if (len == 0 || len > 63) { /* Sanity check for reasonable number length */
374     return false;
375   }
376 
377   char temp[64];
378   memcpy(temp, subject + start, len);
379   temp[len] = '\0';
380 
381   /* Parse as unsigned long */
382   char *endptr;
383   errno = 0;
384   unsigned long value = strtoul(temp, &endptr, 10);
385 
386   if (errno != 0 || endptr == temp || *endptr != '\0') {
387     return false; /* Parse failed */
388   }
389 
390   *out_value = value;
391   return true;
392 }










char
  * asciichat_pcre2_extract_named_group (pcre2_code * regex, pcre2_match_data *
  match_data, const char * group_name, const char * subject)


Extract named substring from PCRE2 match data. Extracts a named
    capture group from match data and returns an allocated string. The caller is
    responsible for freeing the returned string with SAFE_FREE().




Parameters


regex Compiled PCRE2 regex with named groups


match_data Match data from pcre2_match() or pcre2_jit_match()


group_name Name of the capture group to extract


subject Original subject string that was matched


Returns


Allocated string containing the matched substring, or
  NULL if group not matched


Definition at line 264 of file pcre2.c.


265                                                                {
266   if (!regex || !match_data || !group_name || !subject) {
267     log_error("pcre2_extract_named_group: invalid parameters");
268     return NULL;
269   }
270 
271   /* Get group number from name */
272   int group_number = pcre2_substring_number_from_name(regex, (PCRE2_SPTR)group_name);
273   if (group_number < 0) {
274     return NULL; /* Group doesn't exist */
275   }
276 
277   /* Get ovector (output vector with match offsets) */
278   PCRE2_SIZE *ovector = pcre2_get_ovector_pointer(match_data);
279   PCRE2_SIZE start = ovector[2 * group_number];
280   PCRE2_SIZE end = ovector[2 * group_number + 1];
281 
282   if (start == PCRE2_UNSET || end == PCRE2_UNSET) {
283     return NULL; /* Group not matched */
284   }
285 
286   /* Allocate and copy substring */
287   size_t len = end - start;
288   char *result = SAFE_MALLOC(len + 1, char *);
289   if (!result) {
290     log_error("pcre2_extract_named_group: failed to allocate %zu bytes", len + 1);
291     return NULL;
292   }
293 
294   memcpy(result, subject + start, len);
295   result[len] = '\0';
296 
297   return result;
298 }






References SAFE_MALLOC.


Referenced by crypto_regex_extract_gpg_keygrip(),
    crypto_regex_extract_pem_base64(),
    crypto_regex_match_known_hosts(),
    crypto_regex_match_public_key(), and url_parse().






pcre2_singleton_t * asciichat_pcre2_singleton_compile
  (const char * pattern, uint32_t flags)


Compile and cache a PCRE2 regex pattern with thread-safe singleton
    semantics. Allocates a singleton structure and stores the pattern/flags. The
    actual compilation happens lazily on first call to
    asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code().




This function returns immediately without doing expensive regex
    compilation.




Parameters


pattern PCRE2 regex pattern string


flags PCRE2 compile flags


Returns


Opaque singleton handle, or NULL on allocation
  failure


< Per-object lifecycle for lazy compilation


Definition at line 47 of file pcre2.c.


47                                                                                           {
48   if (!pattern) {
49     log_error("PCRE2 singleton: pattern is NULL");
50     return NULL;
51   }
52 
53   /* Allocate singleton structure */
54   pcre2_singleton_t *singleton = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(pcre2_singleton_t), pcre2_singleton_t *);
55   if (!singleton) {
56     log_error("PCRE2 singleton: failed to allocate singleton structure");
57     return NULL;
58   }
59 
60   /* Copy pattern string to avoid stack-use-after-return bugs */
61   size_t pattern_len = strlen(pattern);
62   singleton->pattern = SAFE_MALLOC(pattern_len + 1, char *);
63   if (!singleton->pattern) {
64     log_error("PCRE2 singleton: failed to allocate pattern buffer");
65     SAFE_FREE(singleton);
66     return NULL;
67   }
68   memcpy(singleton->pattern, pattern, pattern_len + 1);
69 
70   /* Initialize fields */
71   singleton->lc = (lifecycle_t)LIFECYCLE_INIT; 
72   atomic_store_u64(&singleton->code, 0);
73   singleton->jit_stack = NULL;
74   singleton->flags = flags;
75 
76   /* Register singleton in global list for automatic cleanup */
77   singleton->next = g_singleton_registry;
78   g_singleton_registry = singleton;
79 
80   /* Mark registry as initialized (one-time per process) */
81   lifecycle_init(&g_registry_lc, "pcre2_registry");
82 
83   return singleton;
84 }






References atomic_store_u64(),
    pcre2_singleton::code, pcre2_singleton::flags,
    pcre2_singleton::jit_stack, pcre2_singleton::lc,
    lifecycle_init(), pcre2_singleton::next,
    pcre2_singleton::pattern, SAFE_FREE, and
  SAFE_MALLOC.


Referenced by grep_init(), log_search_enter_mode(),
    log_search_exit_mode(), log_search_handle_key(), and
    log_search_init().






void
  asciichat_pcre2_singleton_free (pcre2_singleton_t * singleton)


Free a PCRE2 singleton and its resources. Frees the compiled regex
    code, JIT stack, and singleton structure. After calling this, the singleton
    pointer is invalid and should not be used.




Parameters


singleton Handle returned by
  asciichat_pcre2_singleton_compile()


Definition at line 181 of file pcre2.c.


181                                                                   {
182   if (!singleton) {
183     return;
184   }
185 
186   /* Free compiled code if it exists */
187   pcre2_code *code = (pcre2_code *)(uintptr_t)atomic_load_u64(&singleton->code);
188   if (code) {
189     pcre2_code_free(code);
190   }
191 
192   /* Free JIT stack if it exists */
193   if (singleton->jit_stack) {
194     pcre2_jit_stack_free(singleton->jit_stack);
195   }
196 
197   /* Free pattern string */
198   SAFE_FREE(singleton->pattern);
199 
200   /* Free the singleton structure itself */
201   SAFE_FREE(singleton);
202 }






References atomic_load_u64(), pcre2_singleton::code,
    pcre2_singleton::jit_stack, pcre2_singleton::pattern, and
    SAFE_FREE.






pcre2_code
  * asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code (pcre2_singleton_t *
  singleton)


Get the compiled pcre2_code from a singleton handle. Lazily
    compiles the regex on first call. Fast path (subsequent calls) only reads a
    single atomic variable with no synchronization overhead.




Thread-safe: If multiple threads call this concurrently on first
    use, only one will compile; others will wait for the code to become
    non-NULL.




Parameters


singleton Handle returned by
  asciichat_pcre2_singleton_compile()


Returns


Compiled regex code (read-only), or NULL if compilation
  failed


Definition at line 98 of file pcre2.c.


98                                                                              {
99   if (!singleton) {
100     return NULL;
101   }
102 
103   /* Fast path: check if already compiled (lock-free atomic load, no mutex) */
104   pcre2_code *code = (pcre2_code *)(uintptr_t)atomic_load_u64(&singleton->code);
105   if (code != NULL) {
106     return code;
107   }
108 
109   /* Check if compilation already attempted via lifecycle state */
110   if (lifecycle_is_initialized(&singleton->lc)) {
111     /* Already initialized but code is NULL - compilation failed previously */
112     return NULL;
113   }
114 
115   /* Try to win the compilation race using lifecycle_t */
116   if (!lifecycle_init(&singleton->lc, "pcre2_pattern")) {
117     /* Lost race - someone else is compiling or already compiled. Spin for their result. */
118     while ((pcre2_code *)(uintptr_t)atomic_load_u64(&singleton->code) == NULL && lifecycle_is_initialized(&singleton->lc)) {
119       /* Spin-wait for compiler to finish */
120     }
121     return (pcre2_code *)(uintptr_t)atomic_load_u64(&singleton->code);
122   }
123 
124   /* Slow path: we won the race, need to compile. */
125   int errornumber;
126   PCRE2_SIZE erroroffset;
127 
128   code = pcre2_compile((PCRE2_SPTR8)singleton->pattern, PCRE2_ZERO_TERMINATED, singleton->flags, &errornumber,
129                        &erroroffset, NULL);
130 
131   if (!code) {
132     PCRE2_UCHAR error_buf[256];
133     pcre2_get_error_message(errornumber, error_buf, sizeof(error_buf));
134     log_warn("Failed to compile PCRE2 regex at offset %zu: %s", erroroffset, (const char *)error_buf);
135     /* Mark compilation as attempted (failed) by moving to INITIALIZED state */
136     lifecycle_init_commit(&singleton->lc);
137     return NULL;
138   }
139 
140   /* Attempt JIT compilation (non-fatal if fails) */
141   int jit_rc = pcre2_jit_compile(code, PCRE2_JIT_COMPLETE);
142   if (jit_rc < 0) {
143     log_debug("PCRE2 JIT compilation not available (code %d), using interpreted mode", jit_rc);
144   } else {
145     /* Create JIT stack for pattern execution */
146     singleton->jit_stack = pcre2_jit_stack_create(32 * 1024, 512 * 1024, NULL);
147     if (!singleton->jit_stack) {
148       log_warn("Failed to create JIT stack for PCRE2 regex");
149     }
150   }
151 
152   /* Store compiled code (atomic, so subsequent calls see it immediately) */
153   atomic_store_u64(&singleton->code, (uint64_t)(uintptr_t)code);
154   /* Mark compilation as completed by moving to INITIALIZED state */
155   lifecycle_init_commit(&singleton->lc);
156 
157   return code;
158 }






References atomic_load_u64(), atomic_store_u64(),
    pcre2_singleton::code, pcre2_singleton::flags,
    pcre2_singleton::jit_stack, pcre2_singleton::lc,
    lifecycle_init(), lifecycle_init_commit(),
    lifecycle_is_initialized(), log_warn, and
    pcre2_singleton::pattern.


Referenced by grep_highlight(),
    grep_highlight_colored(), grep_init(),
    grep_should_output(), log_search_enter_mode(),
    log_search_exit_mode(), log_search_gather_and_filter_logs(),
    log_search_get_match_info(), and log_search_handle_key().






bool asciichat_pcre2_singleton_is_initialized
  (pcre2_singleton_t * singleton)


Check if a singleton was successfully initialized.


Parameters


singleton Handle returned by
  asciichat_pcre2_singleton_compile()


Returns


true if regex compiled successfully, false
  otherwise


Definition at line 166 of file pcre2.c.


166                                                                             {
167   if (!singleton) {
168     return false;
169   }
170   return (pcre2_code *)(uintptr_t)atomic_load_u64(&singleton->code) != NULL;
171 }

    pcre2_singleton::code.
